name: Card Grid
description: Responsive grid of cards with preset-specific content layouts

product.description: Product cards with image, title, price, rating, add-to-cart button. Grid: 1/2/3/4 cols at sm/md/lg/xl.

slots.card-rating: Star rating row with icon stars and count Badge

content.description: Content/blog cards with thumbnail, title, excerpt, author, date. Grid: 1/2/3 cols.

slots.card-meta: Author avatar, name, and date row

collection.description: Collection/category cards with background image, overlay title, item count. Grid: 2/3 cols.

slots.card-background: Full-bleed background image with gradient overlay
slots.card-overlay-title: Collection name overlaid at bottom with _heading3
slots.card-count: Item count Badge overlaid on image

icon.description: Small icon cards with icon, title, short description. Grid: 2/3/4 cols. Compact.

slots.card-icon: Icon in a rounded muted background circle

capability.description: Capability cards with icon, title, framing paragraph, and 2-4 supporting capability rows or badges. Grid: 1/2/3 cols with wider cards and calmer vertical rhythm.

slots.card-icon: Category icon in a rounded accent-tinted circle or chip.
slots.card-title: Capability category title with heading-scale emphasis and room for two lines when needed.
slots.card-description: Short framing paragraph explaining the category or practice area.
slots.card-supporting-list: Calm vertical list of 2-4 supporting capability rows, each with a label and optional proficiency badge. Use divider-separated rows or a subtle list grouping, not nested mini-cards.

visual_brief: Responsive grid of uniformly-sized cards with consistent gap spacing. Each card is a contained surface with subtle border or shadow, rounded corners (r3), and internal padding. Product preset: top image with fixed aspect ratio, title below in medium-weight text, price in bold heading style, star-rating row with filled/empty star icons, and a full-width add-to-cart button in the card footer. Content preset: thumbnail image, colored category badge, article title, two-line excerpt in muted small text, and an author row with avatar, name, and date. Capability preset: medium-width cards with a category icon, a strong title, one short framing paragraph, and a calm supporting list for two to four related capabilities or badges. The supporting list should feel integrated into the same card surface through spacing, dividers, or a subtle grouping rhythm rather than through a second bordered panel or a stack of mini-cards. Cards maintain equal height within each row via grid stretch when the preset is intended for comparable content density.

layout_hints.card_surface: Each item should feel like one coherent card surface. Do not wrap grid items in an extra shell-level d-surface if the pattern already provides the card treatment.
layout_hints.description_clamp: Allow enough room for meaningful summary copy. In most content grids, descriptions should get at least two to three lines before truncation.
layout_hints.footer_rhythm: Meta rows and CTA rows should align consistently across cards. Footers should not jump vertically between items in the same grid.
layout_hints.hover_behavior: Use CSS-driven hover lift or media scaling rather than inline event handlers that mutate style values in component code.
layout_hints.icon_preset_scope: The icon preset is intentionally compact. Use it for icon + title + short description only, not for longer body copy plus nested capability rows.
layout_hints.capability_preset_scope: When a route needs richer category cards with supporting rows or badges, use the capability preset and keep the grid to one, two, or three columns rather than forcing a compact four-up layout.
layout_hints.nested_surface_avoidance: Capability cards should read as one card surface. Avoid creating a card within a card or wrapping each supporting row in its own mini-panel unless a route explicitly asks for nested grouping.

responsive.mobile: Single column (1 card per row). Cards go full-width with larger touch targets. Image aspect ratio maintained. Gap reduces to gap3.
responsive.tablet: Two columns for most presets. Collection preset stays at 2 columns. Gap at gap4. Cards maintain equal height per row. Capability preset remains at two columns to preserve room for supporting rows.
responsive.desktop: Three to four columns depending on preset. Product goes up to 4 columns at xl breakpoint. Content stays at 3. Icon preset reaches 4 columns. Capability preset tops out at 3 columns so cards can hold a short paragraph plus supporting items without feeling squeezed.

motion.micro: Cards lift on hover with translateY(-2px) and increased shadow over 200ms ease. Image within card scales to 1.03 on hover with overflow hidden. Badge pulses subtly on new items.
motion.transitions: Cards entering viewport fade up from 15px below with staggered 100ms delay per card, 300ms duration. Load-more appends new cards with the same stagger animation.

keyboard: Tab should move through cards and nested actions in reading order.
If the entire card is clickable, expose one clear interactive target instead of conflicting nested links.
announcements: Card titles and supporting metadata should be available to assistive technology without relying on hover-only UI.
accessibility.focus_management: Focused cards should get a clear outline or surface treatment equivalent to hover, and keyboard users should be able to distinguish nested CTAs from the card container.

slots.card-rating: Star rating row with icon stars and count Badge

composition.ProductCard: Card(d-surface, hoverable, lift-on-hover) > [Image(aspect-ratio) + Title(font-medium) + Price(heading4) + RatingRow > [StarIcon[] + CountBadge(d-annotation)] + CTAButton(d-interactive, full-width)]
composition.ContentCard: Card(d-surface) > [Thumbnail + CategoryBadge(d-annotation) + Title(heading4) + Excerpt(text-muted) + MetaRow > [AuthorAvatar + AuthorName + Date]]
composition.CapabilityCard: Card(d-surface, calmer-width) > [IconChip + Title(heading-scale) + Description(text-muted) + SupportingList(divider-separated or subtly grouped) > CapabilityRow*(label + optional proficiency badge)]
